singapore.summary: Drones are increasingly forming the frontline in recent wars, but can these unmanned assets replace their traditional counterparts entirely?

singapore.title: Singapore, Indonesia launch defence alumni initiative
singapore.summary: Singapore Defence Minister Chan Chun Sing - who is in Jakarta for a working visit - and his Indonesian counterpart Sjafrie Sjamsoeddin also reaffirmed their countries’ commitment to explore new areas of bilateral cooperation.

singapore.title: Shanmugam and Tan See Leng to donate Bloomberg damages to charity, say defamation suit was about protecting integrity
singapore.summary: Home Affairs Minister K Shanmugam said allowing Bloomberg "to get away with publishing lies and falsehoods" would set "a new norm" and discourage good people from serving the public.

singapore.summary: They issued separate statements after being awarded $230,000 in damages each by the High Court.

singapore.title: Singapore, Malaysia reaffirm need to 'deepen cooperation' in areas including food security and energy
singapore.summary: President Tharman Shanmugaratnam, who is currently on a state visit to Malaysia, met Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim on Monday (Jul 13), during which they exchanged views on geopolitical developments, including the Middle East situation.

singapore.title: Man linked to 7 road accidents over 4 months to be charged with driving under the influence of etomidate
singapore.summary: The 31-year-old will also be charged with multiple traffic offences, including dangerous driving.

us.title: Kagan, Barrett push Supreme Court budget with boosted security in rare testimony
us.summary: Two Supreme Court justices testified side-by-side before Congress on Tuesday, telling lawmakers that they need more money to defend against the persistent rise in threats targeting themselves and their families. It was a rare showing from Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett, two female justices appointed by presidents of different parties who often…

us.title: 5 takeaways as Supreme Court Justices Barrett and Kagan testify on Capitol Hill
us.summary: Capitol Hill saw an unusual moment on Tuesday as Supreme Court Justices Amy Coney Barrett and Elena Kagan testified before lawmakers. The main reason for their appearance was to make the case for the court’s budget request for the coming year, which includes an increase of roughly $14 million focused on security measures. The hearings…

us.title: Key GOP senator concerned over Blanche involvement in Trump IRS deal
us.summary: Sen. John Cornyn (R-Texas), a key member of the Senate Judiciary Committee, says he is “concerned” about acting Attorney General Todd Blanche’s role in crafting a settlement between President Trump, the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) and Justice Department to shield Trump and his family from IRS audits for years to come. Cornyn said he was…

us.title: ICE shootings spark demands for reform as critics argue agency is ‘stalking Latinos’
us.summary: Two fatal shootings within the span of a week have heightened scrutiny of immigration agents and the leadership of Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin, as Democratic lawmakers argue that new quotas for arrests are leading to violence. In an emotional press conference, members of the Congressional Hispanic Caucus unleashed on U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement…

us.title: FCC approves startup’s space mirror to reflect sunlight to dark parts of Earth
us.summary: The Federal Communications Commission (FCC) approved a startup business’s request to build and launch a mirror satellite that would reflect sunlight back toward the dark parts of Earth. Reflect Orbital of Hawthorne, Calif., received the FCC’s approval on July 9. The company’s goal is to launch an experimental satellite, Eärendil-1, and operate it in low…

us.title: Khanna: ‘I should’ve listened to by wife’ about Platner
us.summary: Rep. Ro Khanna (D-Calif.), who formerly backed ex-Maine senate candidate Graham Platner, said he “should’ve listened to my wife” about the former candidate, who has been accused of sexual assault. Khanna, a progressive, had backed Platner’s campaign during its run, and even attended a rally alongside the former Senate candidate last month. He said the…

us.title: Spain sparkles to shock France 2-0 and advance to the World Cup final
us.summary: Spain is back in the men's World Cup final for the first time since 2010. That's also when Spain won its only World Cup title. France had hoped to reach the final for the third straight tournament.

us.title: How do young people feel about AI? 7 teens weigh in
us.summary: What's it like to grow up and learn in the age of AI? NPR put that question to seven teenagers across the country.

asia.title: DNA tests find blackchin tilapia in cans
asia.summary: DNA tests have confirmed that fish found in canned products seized from a factory in Samut Sakhon were blackchin tilapia, an invasive species, rather than Nile tilapia as the government previously claimed, opposition lawmakers and the BioThai Foundation said on Tuesday.

asia.title: How Beijing’s islands of control are reshaping the South China Sea
asia.summary: A decade after Beijing rejected the ruling by The Hague’s Permanent Court of Arbitration on its South China Sea claims, rival nations continue to manoeuvre for control. In the third of our series on the anniversary, Laura Zhou looks at why the gap between international law and geopolitical reality on the water has never been wider. On a scorching morning this summer, more than a hundred Chinese tourists stepped ashore on Tree Island in the hotly contested Paracels. They were greeted not only by.

asia.title: Commentary: What's behind North Korea's recent economic boom?
asia.summary: North Korea’s recent economic growth is real but it is built on Russian cash, Chinese trade and illicit activity, says this analyst.

asia.title: Fire spurs venue law rethink
asia.summary: Prime Minister Anutin Charnvirakul has pledged a sweeping review of Thailand's entertainment venue laws and zoning regulations following the deadly fire at Rong Beer Na Ladprao.

asia.title: Boko Haram exploited US and Chinese AI chatbots for attacks, Cambridge study finds
asia.summary: Huddled in a room around a big screen, dozens of Boko Haram members listened intently as external consultants, whom they called “the white guys”, coached them on how to use leading artificial intelligence chatbots with laptops pre-installed with VPNs and encryption software. The workshop was one of several training sessions delivered by specialised AI “trainers”, likely members of the vast Islamic State network, to Boko Haram members in northeastern Nigeria sometime during 2023 or 2024,...

asia.title: Spain blanks favoured France to advance to World Cup final
asia.summary: Spain is ​headed back to ⁠the World ⁠Cup final for the ​second time after denying France a third straight appearance following a 2-0 victory in ​a semifinal in Arlington, Texas, on Tuesday.

asia.title: Cambodia aspires to shed its dependencies while staying connected
asia.summary: Connectivity can be an ambiguous term. Different states use it in different ways at different times. In Southeast Asia, as in other regions where regional diplomacy exists in the backdrop of ever-stronger great power disputes, connectivity lies in the conflux of economics, trade necessities and the unyielding pressure of geopolitics. In few countries is this more evident than in Cambodia, where investment in transport logistics has been a political and diplomatic fixture for at least two...

asia.title: ‘Gus’ the T. rex fetches record US$50.1 million at US auction
asia.summary: A Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton nicknamed “Gus” sold on Tuesday for US$50.1 million at Sotheby’s in New York, making it the most valuable dinosaur fossil bought at auction, after a 10-minute battle between seven bidders. “Gus” is one of the world’s most complete T. rex skeletons – with 183 fossilised bones – and was discovered on a cattle ranch in South Dakota in 2021. The giant beast lived some 72 to 66 million years ago – a period characterised by a warm climate, high sea levels and vast...

tech.summary: Microsoft just released a long list of improvements for Windows 11 as part of its bigger patch Tuesdays, and that includes the ability to pause updates indefinitely, as reported earlier by Windows Central. This option rolled out to Windows Insiders earlier this year, allowing you to hold off on updates for up to 35 days […]

The device apparently won't have a screen, but will use a camera and additional sensors to "understand" your environment. The report comes just days after Apple filed a lawsuit against OpenAI that accused […]

briefing: Global markets are on high alert as US President Trump's Hormuz brinkmanship worsens a global fuel crunch, with rising oil prices being only part of the problem, while in Singapore, Defence Minister Chan Chun Sing and his Indonesian counterpart Sjafrie Sjamsoeddin have reaffirmed their commitment to bilateral cooperation. Meanwhile, the High Court has awarded Home Affairs Minister K Shanmugam and Trade Minister Tan See Leng $230,000 in damages each in their defamation suit against Bloomberg, with the ministers stating that the suit was about protecting the integrity of public office. In the technology sector, OpenAI is reportedly set to announce a ChatGPT smart speaker this year, according to a report from Bloomberg, which will allow users to interact with ChatGPT using voice commands.

digests.singapore: Recent news in Singapore has focused on regional cooperation, with Singapore reaffirming its commitment to deepen ties with neighbouring countries, including Indonesia and Malaysia, in areas such as defence, food security, and energy. The country's leaders have also been involved in legal proceedings, with Home Affairs Minister K Shanmugam and Minister Tan See Leng successfully suing Bloomberg for defamation and donating the damages to charity. Meanwhile, a 31-year-old man is set to be charged with driving under the influence of etomidate and multiple traffic offences after being linked to seven road accidents over four months.
digests.us: The US Supreme Court is seeking a budget increase to enhance security measures due to rising threats against justices and their families, as evidenced by the rare testimony of Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett before Congress. Meanwhile, concerns are being raised over the involvement of acting Attorney General Todd Blanche in a settlement between President Trump and the IRS, as well as the actions of US Immigration and Customs Enforcement, which has been criticized for its treatment of Latinos. Additionally, the Federal Communications Commission has approved a startup's plan to launch a satellite that would reflect sunlight to dark parts of the Earth, amidst other developments in US politics and beyond.
digests.asia: The Asia-Pacific region is witnessing significant developments, including China's continued expansion in the South China Sea, with Beijing's islands of control reshaping the geopolitical landscape, while North Korea's economy is experiencing a boom driven by Russian cash, Chinese trade, and illicit activities. In Southeast Asia, Thailand is re-examining its entertainment venue laws after a deadly fire, and Cambodia is seeking to balance its dependencies while staying connected through investments in transport logistics. Meanwhile, regional news is also being shaped by global events, such as the use of AI chatbots by extremist groups like Boko Haram, highlighting the complexities of the region's security and economic landscape.
digests.others: Tensions between the US and Iran continue to escalate, with the Strait of Hormuz emerging as a key point of contention, while the US has also announced plans to withdraw troops from Iraq by September 30, contingent on the disarmament of Iran-backed militias. Meanwhile, in Morocco, authorities have arrested outspoken rapper Mehdi El Youbi, sparking concerns about repression of critical voices and the Gen Z protest movement. International sports and human interest stories are also making headlines, including Spain's victory over France in the World Cup and a dramatic incident on a Ryanair plane.
digests.tech: Recent technology headlines highlight advancements in affordable smartwatches, with the Nothing Watch 3 Pro available for $69, and updates to Windows 11 allowing users to pause updates for longer. Additionally, innovations in AI are emerging, including OpenAI's potential smart speaker and SpaceXAI's Grok programming tool, although the latter has raised concerns over user data privacy. Meanwhile, the use of AI in decision-making is also under scrutiny, with a lawsuit claiming Meta used AI to make layoff decisions, and the US military has deployed explosive drone boats in combat for the first time.
digests.economist: The Economist's analysis highlights the complexities of global technological and economic dominance, particularly in the context of the US-China AI rivalry and the escalating global fuel crunch exacerbated by Trump's policies. Meanwhile, the publication also critiques the financial wisdom of celebrity investment gurus and notes the persistence of social and economic inequalities, such as the scarcity of female breadwinners in America. Additionally, the articles touch on unique cultural and business trends, including Japan's demand for "incident properties" and the marketing prowess of Red Bull.
